title: Stocks fall as Trump comments on war, Intel results awaited
body:
As the US stock market gears up for another day of trading, investors are bracing themselves for a potential downturn, following a record-breaking performance by the S&P 500 and Nasdaq on Wednesday. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq are all expected to decline, with market sentiment dampened by President Trump's recent comments on the ongoing conflict, stating that there is 'no time frame' for its resolution. This uncertainty, combined with a mixed bag of corporate earnings reports, is likely to weigh on investor confidence, leading to a cautious start to the day's trading. Meanwhile, all eyes are on Intel, which is set to release its quarterly results, while Tesla shares are already feeling the heat, slipping in premarket trading after the electric carmaker reported its quarterly earnings after the closing bell on Wednesday.

The market's record-breaking run on Wednesday, which saw the S&P 500 and Nasdaq reach new highs, is unlikely to be sustained, at least in the short term. With the President's comments on the conflict introducing a new layer of uncertainty, investors are adopting a wait-and-see approach, hesitant to make any bold moves. This caution is reflected in the futures market, where the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq are all pointing to a lower opening. As the day unfolds, market participants will be closely watching the Intel earnings report, which is expected to provide valuable insights into the tech sector's performance. A strong showing from Intel could help mitigate some of the losses, but it remains to be seen whether the chipmaker's results will be enough to offset the broader market jitters.

The Tesla earnings report, released after the market close on Wednesday, has already had a significant impact on the company's shares, which are slipping in premarket trading. While the electric carmaker's results were broadly in line with expectations, investors are taking a closer look at the company's guidance and outlook, which may have fallen short of some analysts' estimates. As one of the most closely watched companies in the tech sector, Tesla's performance has a significant impact on market sentiment, and its shares are often seen as a bellwether for the broader industry. With the company's stock already under pressure, any further weakness could have a ripple effect on the rest of the market.

The ongoing conflict, and the President's comments on its resolution, are likely to remain a major concern for investors in the coming days. The lack of a clear timeline for its ending has introduced a new layer of uncertainty, making it difficult for market participants to predict the outcome. As a result, investors are adopting a risk-off approach, seeking safer havens such as bonds and other low-risk assets. This shift in sentiment could have a significant impact on the market, leading to a rotation out of stocks and into other asset classes. With the market already on edge, any further developments on the conflict front could exacerbate the decline, leading to a more pronounced sell-off.

As the day unfolds, investors will be closely watching the Intel earnings report, as well as any further developments on the conflict front. The tech sector, in particular, will be under scrutiny, with Intel's results providing a valuable insight into the industry's performance. While the market's decline may be short-lived, the underlying uncertainty and volatility are likely to persist, making it a challenging environment for investors to navigate. With the market's record-breaking run on Wednesday now a distant memory, investors are bracing themselves for a more turbulent ride, at least in the short term.

Looking ahead, the next few days will be crucial in determining the market's direction. With a slew of corporate earnings reports still to come, investors will be closely watching the results, seeking any clues on the health of the US economy. The conflict, and the President's comments on its resolution, will also remain a major concern, with any further developments having the potential to significantly impact market sentiment. As the market navigates this uncertain environment, investors will need to remain vigilant, adapting to the changing landscape and adjusting their strategies accordingly. With the market's volatility showing no signs of abating, one thing is certain – the next few days will be critical in shaping the market's trajectory, and investors will need to be prepared for anything.
